title: rubus podophyllos var. vestitiformis
intro: Rubus podophyllos var. vestitiformis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the raspberry species found primarily in the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The health benefits of Rubus podophyllos var. vestitiformis include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain diseases, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, are made by using the leaves and roots of the plant, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
